subject: Geography
title: Polar Vortex
description: A quick win: understand Polar Vortex.

hook.text: The polar vortex is a band of cold wind circling the Arctic that keeps frigid air contained.

buildup.text: When the vortex is strong, cold air stays locked over the pole and mid-latitudes stay mild.

discovery.text: When it weakens, Arctic air spills south—causing extreme cold snaps in the US, Europe, and Asia.

twist.text: Arctic warming may be destabilizing the vortex, making extreme winter weather more frequent.

climax.text: Paradoxically, a warming Arctic could mean colder winters in New York and London.

punchline.text: Warming the Arctic could freeze the rest of us.

quiz.question: What happens when the polar vortex weakens?
options: Arctic air spills south, causing extreme cold snaps
